research.status_summary: Actively operating and hiring — no layoffs or acquisition signals; company reported its biggest Q1 and biggest March in company history as of early 2026.
research.hiring_policy: Stampli is actively adding staff (new hires in Sales, Customer Success, and Talent Acquisition as of Mar 2026), but no public evidence of visa sponsorship or EOR-friendly international hiring; roles appear US-based and the careers page does not mention remote-international or EOR arrangements.
recent_news: Launched Stampli Deep Finance™ on March 31, 2026 — an agentic AI layer that turns invoice/AP data into consultant-grade executive spend intelligence for CFOs and Controllers, surfacing vendor risk, spending shifts, and savings opportunities in minutes.
Recognized by Software Advice FrontRunners for Best Customer Satisfaction in Accounts Payable in 2026, continuing a streak of G2 and TrustRadius top-rated awards.
Doubled Procure-to-Pay wins QoQ in Q1 2026 per internal LinkedIn post from a sales leader, with Deep Finance cited as a key differentiator driving platform adoption over point solutions.
research.growth_signal: Headcount stable at ~302–303 (PitchBook/Tracxn, Feb–2026), no new funding round since $61M Series D (Oct 2023), but strong product velocity with Deep Finance launch and record Q1 revenue suggesting organic growth without fresh capital.
research.fit_for_lior: Weak fit for Lior — Stampli is a US-headquartered B2B fintech with no visible Israeli R&D hub, no EOR/international hiring signal, and its open roles skew toward US-based Sales and Customer Success rather than Creative AI, Product Analyst, or Vibe Coder profiles.
